body: The portal page goes blank right after I enter my password. Tried Chrome and Safari.
TRIAGE: Caching issue on CDN edge node. Escalated to hosting team — expect resolution within 4 hours.
Hi Lani, we've identified a CDN caching issue. Please try a hard refresh (Cmd+Shift+R) — it should load now.
The June schedule page still shows May 3 and May 17 as session dates. Very confusing for new volunteers.
TRIAGE: Schedule feed not refreshing from backend. Noelani to update the content CMS directly as workaround.
Could you send me the PDF for my March reef monitoring training completion? I need it for a job application.
Hi Hina, I've attached your certificate. Let me know if you need a signed copy from Dr. Kamaka.
Three of our volunteers did the full 3-hour session on June 1 but none of them see hours on their profiles.
TRIAGE: Session sign-in sheet was submitted but the hours sync job failed. I'll manually credit the hours today.
Hours are showing now — thank you Noelani!
I can't find the current MOU template anywhere on the partner resources page. We need it to renew our data agreement.
TRIAGE: MOU template was accidentally removed during a resources page update. Recovering from backup now.
Hi! I just signed up as a volunteer. How do I register for the Kāneʻohe Bay monitoring rotation?
Welcome, Lani! Go to Volunteer Dashboard → Monitoring Schedule → Register. Sessions are 1st and 3rd Saturday 9–12.
The pre-dive safety video gives a 504 timeout error on every attempt. Three new volunteers can't complete orientation.
TRIAGE: Video host server overloaded. Switched CDN region — please try again now.
Works now — all three volunteers have completed the safety briefing. Mahalo!
The Q1 2026 monitoring report PDF link returns a 404. We need it for our board presentation.
Restored the report link. Here it is: https://partners.coralfuturesinstitute.org/reports/q1-2026. Marking solved.
The site map is showing old 2025 marker positions. The northern transect line moved 200m this season.
TRIAGE: GIS layer not updated with new 2026 coordinates. David is pushing the updated shapefile now.
I'm a PhD student at Seaview working with Dr. Carter. Can I get read access to the 2020–2024 coral baseline dataset?
TRIAGE: Forwarded to Dr. Kamaka for approval. Dataset access requires a signed data agreement — emailing template now.
Both field tablets are drifting 10–15m on GPS. Noticed it during yesterday's transect run near Kapapa Island.
TRIAGE: Tablets due for GPS antenna recalibration — last done December 2025. Kaipo, bring them in Monday before the dive.
I can't find the 2026 monitoring protocol PDF anywhere on the resources page.
It's under Resources → Protocols → 2026. Direct link: https://volunteers.coralfuturesinstitute.org/protocols/2026.
My account hit the 200-photo limit mid-survey. Is there any way to get a temporary increase for intensive survey weeks?
Temporarily raised your limit to 500 for June. I'll flag to Dr. Kamaka about increasing the standard limit for survey weeks.
The 'Get Started' button in my welcome email goes to a 404 page.
Fixed — the link was pointing to a staging URL. Correct link: https://volunteers.coralfuturesinstitute.org/start. Marking solved.
The June 21 calendar invite says 10 AM but we always start at 9 AM sharp at the boat ramp.
Corrected — resent the invite with 9 AM start. Sorry for the confusion, Keoni!
